title: TPM Attestation Failure — Known Error Code
description: A TPM-attestation-related error code documented in the Microsoft Autopilot known-issues list was observed (0x800705B4, 0x801C03EA, 0x81039001, 0x81039023, 0x81039024, 0x80070490). These block self-deploying and pre-provisioning deployments during 'Securing your hardware'. Observability: attestation precedes MDM enrollment, so in the normal deployment model (agent installed via IME after enrollment) these codes are rarely visible — the rule primarily serves bootstrap-token deployments. See docs/agent/autopilot-ztd-diagnostics.md (Observability section).
author: Autopilot Monitor
explanation:
A TPM attestation error code from Microsoft's Autopilot known-issues list was observed. These codes block self-deploying mode and pre-provisioning during the **Securing your hardware** step.

Code meanings:

- **0x800705B4** — timeout; the most common cause in self-deploying mode is hardware that is not TPM 2.0 capable (e.g. a virtual machine). Self-deploying mode cannot be used on such devices.
- **0x801C03EA** — TPM attestation failed, so the Microsoft Entra join with a device token failed.
- **0x81039001** — E_AUTOPILOT_CLIENT_TPM_MAX_ATTESTATION_RETRY_EXCEEDED; intermittent — a retry of the deployment often succeeds.
- **0x81039023** — Windows 11 attestation failure; fixed by cumulative update KB5013943 (21H2) or later.
- **0x81039024** — the TPM firmware has known vulnerabilities; attestation is refused until the firmware is updated.
- **0x80070490** — AMD platforms with ASP firmware TPM; fixed in later AMD firmware releases.

TPM attestation additionally requires access to `*.microsoftaik.azure.net` and, for firmware TPMs, the vendor EK-certificate endpoints. A real-time clock that is off by several minutes also causes attestation failures (see the session's ntp_time_check event).

remediation.title: Match the code to its documented fix
steps: 0x800705B4: verify the device has TPM 2.0 (self-deploying/pre-provisioning is unsupported on VMs).
0x81039001: retry the deployment — this code is documented as intermittent.
0x81039023: install KB5013943 (Windows 11 21H2) or a later cumulative update into the image.
0x81039024 / 0x80070490: update the TPM/system firmware from the OEM.
Verify network access to *.microsoftaik.azure.net and the TPM vendor EK-certificate URL (Intel/Qualcomm/AMD).
Check the session's ntp_time_check event — a clock offset of minutes breaks attestation (w32tm /resync /force).

relatedDocs.title: Microsoft Learn: Windows Autopilot known issues
Microsoft Learn: Windows Autopilot requirements — Networking (TPM attestation endpoints)
